ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS MOOT, IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (CCP SEC. 430.10(E) AND (F).) AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLEARLY ALLEGE WHAT JUDICIAL DETERMINATION IS SOUGHT. AS PLED, IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY EXISTS. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLEARLY IDENTIFY THE SPECIFIC PROVISIONS OF THE POLICY THAT WERE ALLEGEDLY BREACHED. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BAD FAITH, PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLEARLY ALLEGE THAT BENEFITS DUE UNDER THE POLICY HAVE BEEN WITHHELD AND THAT THE REASON FOR WITHHOLDING THE BENEFITS WAS UNREASONABLE OR WITHOUT PROPER CAUSE. (LOVE V. FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE (1990) 221 CAL.APP.3D 1136, 1151.) THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DOES NOT ATTACH THE POLICY OR STATE THE RELEVANT PROVISIONS OF THE POLICY, AND THEREFORE IT DOES NOT ADEQUATELY PLEAD THAT BENEFITS WERE IMPROPERLY WITHHELD UNDER THE POLICY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Defendant Aaa Northern California, Nevada & Utah Insurance'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. EMMANUEL ENYINWA, Tel No. (415) 956-6100 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. GINA J. BELTRAMO of Coddington, Hicks & Danforth, Tel No. (650) 592-5400 appearing telephonically for Defendant AAA Northern California, Nevada and Utah Insurance Exchange.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA AND UTAH INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S Demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend because the Second Amended Complaint fails to state a cause of action for declaratory relief and the defect cannot be cured by further amendment. There is no controversy as to the burdens of either party, but rather the controversy surrounds whether Defendant complied with its statutory obligations. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied as moot, in light of the ruling on demurrer. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e) and (f).) As to the first cause of action for declaratory relief, Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to clearly allege what judicial determination is sought. As pled, it is unclear whether an actual controversy exists. As to the second cause of action for breach of contract, Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to clearly identify the specific provisions of the policy that were allegedly breached. As to the third cause of action for bad faith, Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to clearly allege that benefits due under the policy have been withheld and that the reason for withholding the benefits was unreasonable or without proper cause. (Love v. Fire Insurance Exchange (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1136, 1151.) The First Amended Complaint does not attach the policy or state the relevant provisions of the policy, and therefore it does not adequately plead that benefits were improperly withheld under the policy. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
